tests.name: Neer Test
tests.method: Examiner stabilizes scapula while passively elevating the arm into flexion with internal rotation. Pain indicates possible impingement.

tests.name: Hawkins-Kennedy Test
tests.method: Shoulder flexed to 90° and elbow flexed to 90°. Examiner internally rotates the shoulder. Pain suggests impingement.

tests.name: Empty Can (Jobe) Test
tests.method: Patient abducts arm ~30–45° forward with thumb pointing down. Examiner applies downward resistance.

tests.name: Full Can Test
tests.purpose: Assesses supraspinatus function with less impingement
tests.method: Patient elevates arm in scapular plane with thumb up against resistance.

tests.name: Drop Arm Test
tests.method: Patient lowers arm slowly from 90° abduction. Inability to control movement suggests tear.

tests.name: Speed's Test
tests.purpose: Assesses long head of biceps tendon
tests.method: Patient flexes shoulder against resistance with elbow extended and forearm supinated.

tests.name: Yergason's Test
tests.method: Patient supinates forearm against resistance while examiner palpates bicipital groove.

tests.name: Apprehension Test
tests.method: Shoulder placed in abduction and external rotation. Apprehension or fear indicates instability.

tests.name: Relocation Test
tests.method: Posterior pressure applied to humeral head while in apprehension position. Reduction of symptoms supports instability.

tests.name: O'Brien Active Compression Test
tests.purpose: Detects SLAP lesion or AC pathology
tests.method: Arm flexed 90°, adducted, internally rotated; downward resistance applied.

tests.name: Cross Body Adduction Test
tests.method: Patient actively moves arm across chest. Pain at AC joint indicates pathology.

tests.name: Cozen's Test
tests.method: Patient extends wrist against resistance with elbow extended. Pain at lateral epicondyle is positive.

tests.name: Mill's Test
tests.method: Examiner passively pronates forearm, flexes wrist, and extends elbow. Pain indicates irritation.

tests.name: Golfer's Elbow Test
tests.method: Wrist flexion and pronation performed against resistance. Medial elbow pain suggests pathology.

tests.name: Valgus Stress Test
tests.method: Valgus force applied while elbow slightly flexed. Excessive opening indicates injury.

tests.name: Varus Stress Test
tests.method: Varus force applied to elbow. Increased laxity suggests injury.

tests.name: Phalen's Test
tests.method: Patient presses dorsal surfaces of hands together with wrists flexed for 30–60 seconds.

tests.name: Reverse Phalen's Test
tests.method: Patient holds wrist extension position. Symptoms suggest carpal tunnel involvement.

tests.name: Tinel's Sign
tests.method: Examiner taps over median nerve at carpal tunnel. Tingling indicates irritation.

tests.name: Finkelstein Test
tests.method: Thumb placed inside fist, wrist deviated toward ulna. Pain indicates positive test.

tests.name: Grind Test
tests.method: Axial compression and rotation applied to thumb metacarpal. Pain/crepitus suggests arthritis.

tests.name: Spurling's Test
tests.method: Neck extended, rotated, and compressed. Reproduction of arm symptoms indicates nerve root involvement.

tests.name: Cervical Distraction Test
tests.purpose: Tests symptom relief from nerve compression
tests.method: Gentle traction applied to cervical spine. Decreased symptoms suggest nerve root compression.

tests.name: Upper Limb Tension Test (ULTT)
tests.method: Upper limb positioned progressively to tension neural structures. Symptoms indicate neural involvement.

tests.name: Rib Spring Test
tests.method: Posterior pressure applied over ribs while patient prone. Pain or hypomobility indicates dysfunction.

tests.name: Straight Leg Raise (SLR)
tests.method: Patient supine; examiner raises straight leg. Radiating pain between 30–70° suggests neural involvement.

tests.name: Crossed SLR
tests.method: Raising unaffected leg reproduces symptoms on opposite side.

tests.name: Slump Test
tests.method: Patient sits slumped, flexes neck, extends knee, and dorsiflexes ankle. Symptoms indicate neural sensitivity.

tests.name: Quadrant Test
tests.method: Patient performs lumbar extension combined with rotation and side bending.

tests.name: FABER Test
tests.purpose: Differentiates hip and SI joint pathology
tests.method: Hip placed in flexion, abduction, external rotation; downward pressure applied.

tests.name: Compression Test
tests.method: Patient side lying; downward compression applied through pelvis. Pain indicates SI involvement.

tests.name: Thigh Thrust Test
tests.method: Hip flexed to 90°; posterior force applied through femur.

tests.name: FADIR Test
tests.method: Hip flexion, adduction, and internal rotation performed. Pain indicates impingement.

tests.name: Thomas Test
tests.method: Patient holds one hip to chest while opposite thigh position is observed.

tests.name: Ober's Test
tests.method: Side-lying hip extension and abduction followed by controlled adduction.

tests.name: Trendelenburg Test
tests.method: Patient stands on one leg. Pelvic drop indicates gluteus medius weakness.

tests.name: Lachman Test
tests.method: Knee flexed 20–30°. Examiner pulls tibia anteriorly. Increased translation indicates ACL injury.

tests.name: Anterior Drawer Test
tests.method: Knee flexed 90°. Tibia pulled anteriorly. Excess movement suggests ACL injury.

tests.name: Posterior Drawer Test
tests.method: Tibia pushed posteriorly at 90° flexion. Excess movement indicates PCL injury.

tests.name: McMurray Test
tests.method: Knee rotated while flexing/extending. Pain or clicking indicates meniscal pathology.

tests.name: Thessaly Test
tests.method: Patient stands on one leg with knee flexed and rotates body. Pain/catching suggests meniscus injury.

tests.name: Valgus Stress Test
tests.method: Valgus force applied at 0° and 30° knee flexion.

tests.name: Varus Stress Test
tests.method: Varus force applied at knee.

tests.name: Patellar Grind Test
tests.method: Downward pressure applied to patella during quadriceps contraction.

tests.name: Anterior Drawer Test
tests.method: Talus translated forward while stabilizing tibia. Excess motion indicates ligament injury.

tests.name: Talar Tilt Test
tests.method: Inversion stress applied to ankle. Excess tilt indicates injury.

tests.name: Thompson Test
tests.method: Calf squeezed while patient prone. Absence of plantar flexion indicates rupture.

tests.name: Kleiger External Rotation Test
tests.method: External rotation force applied to foot. Pain indicates high ankle sprain.

tests.name: Windlass Test
tests.method: Great toe dorsiflexed while plantar fascia tension increases. Heel pain indicates positive test.

tests.name: Navicular Drop Test
tests.method: Navicular height measured seated and standing. Difference indicates excessive pronation.

tests.name: Too Many Toes Sign
tests.method: Observe foot from behind during standing. Visible toes indicate deformity.

tests.name: Manual Muscle Testing (MMT)
tests.purpose: Evaluates muscle strength using a standardized grading system (0–5)
tests.method: Patient performs specific movement while examiner applies resistance. Strength is graded from no contraction (0) to normal strength (5).

tests.name: Medical Research Council (MRC) Scale
tests.purpose: Standard strength grading system for neurological patients
tests.method: Each muscle group is tested against gravity and resistance and scored 0–5.

tests.name: Motor Control Assessment
tests.method: Patient performs isolated movements while examiner observes abnormal synergies or compensations.

tests.name: Modified Ashworth Scale (MAS)
tests.purpose: Measures resistance to passive movement associated with spasticity
tests.method: Examiner moves limb passively through ROM and grades resistance from 0–4.

tests.name: Modified Tardieu Scale
tests.method: Limb moved at different velocities while assessing muscle reaction angle.

tests.name: Pendulum Test (Wartenberg)
tests.purpose: Evaluates lower limb tone and spasticity
tests.method: Patient seated with leg relaxed; examiner observes oscillations after knee extension.

tests.name: Biceps Reflex (C5–C6)
tests.purpose: Tests upper limb deep tendon reflex
tests.method: Tap biceps tendon while elbow is relaxed; observe elbow flexion response.

tests.name: Triceps Reflex (C7–C8)
tests.method: Tap triceps tendon above elbow; observe extension response.

tests.name: Patellar Reflex (L3–L4)
tests.purpose: Evaluates femoral nerve and spinal segments
tests.method: Tap patellar tendon; observe knee extension.

tests.name: Achilles Reflex (S1–S2)
tests.purpose: Evaluates tibial nerve and sacral segments
tests.method: Tap Achilles tendon; observe plantar flexion.

tests.name: Babinski Sign
tests.method: Stroke lateral sole of foot; observe great toe response.

tests.name: Hoffmann Sign
tests.method: Flick distal phalanx of middle finger and observe thumb/index response.

tests.name: Light Touch Test
tests.method: Apply light touch using cotton or tissue and compare sides.

tests.name: Pin Prick Test
tests.method: Apply sharp stimulus and compare symmetrical areas.

tests.name: Temperature Sensation
tests.method: Apply warm/cold stimulus and compare responses.

tests.name: Vibration Sense
tests.method: Place vibrating tuning fork on bony prominences.

tests.name: Proprioception Test
tests.method: Move finger/toe upward or downward and ask patient to identify direction.

tests.name: Dermatome Testing
tests.purpose: Maps sensory loss according to nerve roots
tests.method: Test sensation in specific skin regions corresponding to spinal levels.

tests.name: Myotome Testing
tests.purpose: Evaluates motor function of nerve roots
tests.method: Test key movements associated with cervical/lumbar roots.

tests.name: Finger-to-Nose Test
tests.method: Patient touches nose then examiner's finger repeatedly. Observe accuracy and tremor.

tests.name: Heel-to-Shin Test
tests.method: Patient slides heel along opposite shin while lying down.

tests.name: Rapid Alternating Movement Test
tests.method: Patient rapidly alternates hand movements.

tests.name: Finger-to-Finger Test
tests.method: Patient touches examiner's finger and own nose alternately.

tests.name: Romberg Test
tests.method: Patient stands feet together with eyes open then closed. Increased sway indicates impairment.

tests.name: Sharpened Romberg Test
tests.method: Patient stands heel-to-toe with eyes closed.

tests.name: Single Leg Stance Test
tests.method: Patient stands on one leg while time is recorded.

tests.name: Functional Reach Test
tests.method: Patient reaches forward without stepping; distance is measured.

tests.name: Observational Gait Analysis
tests.method: Observe gait phases, symmetry, speed, and compensations.

tests.name: 10-Meter Walk Test
tests.method: Patient walks 10 meters at comfortable/maximal speed; time recorded.

tests.name: 6-Minute Walk Test
tests.method: Patient walks maximum distance in 6 minutes.

tests.name: Dynamic Gait Index (DGI)
tests.purpose: Evaluates gait adaptability and fall risk
tests.method: Patient performs walking tasks including head turns and speed changes.

tests.name: Berg Balance Scale (BBS)
tests.method: Patient performs 14 balance tasks scored 0–4.

tests.name: Functional Independence Measure (FIM)
tests.method: Rates independence in mobility, self-care, cognition, and communication.

tests.name: Timed Up and Go (TUG)
tests.purpose: Measures functional mobility and fall risk
tests.method: Patient rises from chair, walks 3 meters, turns, returns, and sits. Time recorded.

tests.name: Fugl-Meyer Assessment (FMA)
tests.method: Tests movement, reflexes, coordination, sensation, and balance.

tests.name: NIH Stroke Scale (NIHSS)
tests.method: Assesses consciousness, vision, motor function, sensation, language, and neglect.

tests.name: Modified Rankin Scale (mRS)
tests.method: Rates functional independence from 0 (no symptoms) to 6 (death).

tests.name: ASIA Impairment Scale (AIS)
tests.purpose: Classifies severity of spinal cord injury
tests.method: Tests key sensory points, motor muscles, and neurological level.

tests.name: ISNCSCI Examination
tests.method: Systematic sensory and motor testing to determine injury level.

tests.name: Denver Developmental Screening Test II (DDST-II)
tests.purpose: Screens developmental delays in children 0–6 years
tests.method: Child performs age-appropriate tasks in personal-social, fine motor, language, and gross motor domains.

tests.name: Alberta Infant Motor Scale (AIMS)
tests.purpose: Assesses gross motor development in infants
tests.method: Infant observed in prone, supine, sitting, standing positions; motor skills scored based on age norms.

tests.name: Bayley Scales of Infant Development
tests.method: Evaluates cognitive, motor, and language skills through structured tasks.

tests.name: Gross Motor Function Measure (GMFM-88/66)
tests.purpose: Measures motor function in children with cerebral palsy
tests.method: Child performs lying, rolling, sitting, crawling, standing, walking tasks.

tests.name: Gross Motor Function Classification System (GMFCS)
tests.purpose: Classifies severity of motor disability in CP
tests.method: Observational classification from Level I (walks independently) to Level V (severely limited).

tests.name: Modified Ashworth Scale (Pediatric use)
tests.method: Passive movement of limbs; resistance graded 0–4.

tests.name: Tardieu Scale
tests.method: Passive movement at different speeds; angle of muscle reaction recorded.

tests.name: Pediatric Tone Assessment
tests.method: Observation of resistance, posture, and handling response.

tests.name: Moro Reflex
tests.method: Sudden head drop or noise; observe arm abduction and extension.

tests.name: Asymmetrical Tonic Neck Reflex (ATNR)
tests.method: Turn infant's head; observe extension on face side, flexion on skull side.

tests.name: Symmetrical Tonic Neck Reflex (STNR)
tests.method: Neck flexion/extension causes limb flexion/extension changes.

tests.name: Tonic Labyrinthine Reflex (TLR)
tests.method: Supine increases extensor tone, prone increases flexor tone.

tests.name: Palmar Grasp Reflex
tests.method: Object placed in palm triggers finger flexion.

tests.name: Righting Reactions
tests.method: Child tilted; body adjusts to maintain midline.

tests.name: Protective Extension Reactions
tests.method: Child pushed off balance; arms extend to protect fall.

tests.name: Equilibrium Reactions
tests.method: Controlled perturbation while child maintains posture.

tests.name: Peabody Developmental Motor Scales (PDMS-2)
tests.purpose: Measures fine and gross motor skills
tests.method: Child performs grasping, object manipulation, locomotion tasks.

tests.name: Pediatric Balance Scale
tests.method: 14 tasks similar to Berg Balance Scale adapted for pediatrics.

tests.name: Timed Up and Go (Pediatric version)
tests.method: Child stands, walks, turns, returns to chair; time recorded.

tests.name: MACS (Manual Ability Classification System)
tests.method: Rates manual ability in daily activities from Level I–V.

tests.name: CFCS (Communication Function Classification System)
tests.method: Rates effectiveness of communication from Level I–V.

tests.name: EDACS (Eating and Drinking Ability Classification System)
tests.method: Classifies safety and efficiency of eating/drinking.

tests.name: Selective Control Assessment of the Lower Extremity (SCALE)
tests.method: Child performs isolated joint movements of hip, knee, ankle.

tests.name: Hammersmith Infant Neurological Examination (HINE)
tests.method: Evaluates cranial nerves, posture, tone, reflexes, and motor development.

tests.name: Observational Gait Scale
tests.method: Clinician observes walking pattern including foot contact and knee position.

tests.name: 6-Minute Walk Test (Pediatric use)
tests.method: Child walks as far as possible in 6 minutes.

tests.name: Primitive Reflex Integration Assessment
tests.method: Tests whether reflexes like ATNR or Moro are retained beyond normal age.

tests.name: Timed Up and Go (TUG)
tests.purpose: Assesses functional mobility, balance, and fall risk
tests.method: Patient starts seated, stands up, walks 3 meters, turns, walks back, and sits. Time is recorded.

tests.name: 10-Meter Walk Test (10MWT)
tests.purpose: Measures comfortable and fast walking speed
tests.method: Patient walks 10 meters; time is measured over the middle section to calculate gait speed.

tests.name: 6-Minute Walk Test (6MWT)
tests.purpose: Evaluates walking endurance and functional exercise capacity
tests.method: Patient walks continuously for 6 minutes on a measured course. Distance is recorded.

tests.name: 2-Minute Walk Test (2MWT)
tests.purpose: Short endurance assessment for limited mobility patients
tests.method: Patient walks as far as possible for 2 minutes. Distance recorded.

tests.name: Functional Ambulation Category (FAC)
tests.method: Clinician observes patient walking and assigns level based on assistance required.

tests.name: Rivermead Mobility Index (RMI)
tests.purpose: Measures mobility level after neurological injury
tests.method: Patient performs graded mobility tasks from turning in bed to running.

tests.name: Five Times Sit-to-Stand Test (5xSTS)
tests.purpose: Assesses lower limb strength and functional transfer ability
tests.method: Patient stands up and sits down 5 times as quickly as possible without using arms. Time recorded.

tests.name: 30-Second Chair Stand Test
tests.purpose: Measures functional lower limb strength and endurance
tests.method: Patient completes maximum sit-to-stand repetitions in 30 seconds.

tests.name: Transfer Assessment
tests.method: Observe bed mobility, sit-to-stand, chair transfer, and safety.

tests.name: Berg Balance Scale (BBS)
tests.purpose: Measures static and dynamic balance and fall risk
tests.method: Patient performs 14 balance tasks scored from 0–4 (maximum 56 points).

tests.name: Functional Reach Test
tests.method: Patient reaches forward as far as possible without stepping. Distance measured.

tests.name: Tinetti Performance Oriented Mobility Assessment (POMA)
tests.purpose: Evaluates balance and gait-related fall risk
tests.method: Patient performs sitting, standing, walking, and turning tasks scored by examiner.

tests.name: Mini-BESTest
tests.method: Patient performs anticipatory, reactive, sensory, and gait balance tasks.

tests.name: Four Square Step Test (FSST)
tests.purpose: Evaluates dynamic balance and stepping ability
tests.method: Patient steps over four squares in different directions while time is recorded.

tests.name: Single Leg Stance Test
tests.method: Patient stands on one leg while maintaining balance; duration recorded.

tests.name: Observational Gait Analysis
tests.method: Clinician observes gait phases, alignment, symmetry, and assistive device use.

tests.name: Dynamic Gait Index (DGI)
tests.purpose: Assesses ability to modify gait safely
tests.method: Patient walks while changing speed, turning head, stepping over obstacles, and changing direction.

tests.name: Functional Gait Assessment (FGA)
tests.method: Patient performs 10 walking tasks including narrow base walking and stair negotiation.

tests.name: Barthel Index
tests.purpose: Measures independence in basic daily activities
tests.method: Rates feeding, bathing, grooming, dressing, bowel/bladder control, transfers, and mobility.

tests.name: Katz ADL Index
tests.method: Evaluates bathing, dressing, toileting, transferring, continence, and feeding.

tests.name: Functional Independence Measure (FIM)
tests.purpose: Measures burden of care and independence
tests.method: Rates 18 items covering motor and cognitive function.

tests.name: Modified Barthel Index
tests.method: Similar to Barthel with expanded scoring system.

tests.name: Lawton IADL Scale
tests.method: Assesses shopping, cooking, transportation, medication, finances, and housekeeping.

tests.name: Frenchay Activities Index
tests.purpose: Measures participation in domestic and social activities
tests.method: Patient reports frequency of daily and social activities.

tests.name: Morse Fall Scale
tests.purpose: Identifies risk of falling in clinical settings
tests.method: Scores history of falls, mobility, IV therapy, gait, mental status, and assistance needs.

tests.name: Hendrich II Fall Risk Model
tests.purpose: Predicts fall risk in hospitalized patients
tests.method: Assesses confusion, depression, elimination, dizziness, medications, and mobility.

tests.name: STRATIFY Scale
tests.method: Evaluates previous falls, cognition, vision, mobility, and toileting.

tests.name: 6-Minute Walk Test
tests.method: Patient walks maximum distance for 6 minutes.

tests.name: Incremental Shuttle Walk Test
tests.method: Patient walks between markers at increasing speeds following audio cues.

tests.name: Borg Rating of Perceived Exertion (RPE)
tests.method: Patient rates effort level from 6–20 or 0–10 scale during activity.

tests.name: WHO Disability Assessment Schedule (WHODAS 2.0)
tests.method: Patient questionnaire covering cognition, mobility, self-care, relationships, life activities, and participation.

tests.name: SF-36 Health Survey
tests.method: Patient questionnaire covering physical, emotional, and social domains.

tests.name: EQ-5D
tests.method: Patient rates mobility, self-care, activities, pain, and psychological status.

tests.name: Patient-Specific Functional Scale (PSFS)
tests.method: Patient identifies difficult activities and rates difficulty from 0–10.

tests.name: Return to Sport Assessment
tests.method: Includes strength, balance, agility, functional tests, and sport-specific tasks.

tests.name: DASH (Disabilities of Arm, Shoulder and Hand)
tests.method: Patient questionnaire about symptoms and ability to perform activities.

tests.name: QuickDASH
tests.method: 11-item questionnaire assessing upper limb function.

tests.name: Lower Extremity Functional Scale (LEFS)
tests.method: Patient rates difficulty with daily and physical activities.

tests.name: WOMAC Index
tests.purpose: Assesses hip and knee osteoarthritis symptoms
tests.method: Evaluates pain, stiffness, and physical function.
